name: post_delivery_feedback_not_surfaced
description: Army client (2 Corps Supply) — delivery confirmed and payment received within the same week. Order is fully closed. No feedback message from Ashish to client visible anywhere. Agent should surface a post-delivery feedback follow-up task as a cadence implicit task.
expected_output: Parent task: 2 Corps Supply order — Status: Completed (delivery 20 Mar, payment 22 Mar). Implicit task: Post-delivery feedback follow-up with 2 Corps Supply — payment received, order closed, no feedback message sent. Next step: Ashish to follow up with 2 Corps Supply to confirm satisfaction with delivery and quality — standard relationship step after Army client order completion.
pass_criteria: PASS: Agent surfaces a post-delivery feedback or satisfaction follow-up task as an implicit/cadence task triggered by order completion (payment received). FAIL: Agent marks the order as complete with no forward-looking relationship task — treating payment received as the absolute final state. FAIL: Agent notes GST reconciliation or other administrative steps but omits client feedback follow-up entirely.
challenge: Payment received is a powerful task-closure signal. 'Received, thank you sir' is a natural conversation endpoint. The agent must recognise that order closure triggers a relationship follow-up step (feedback) that is almost never written in WhatsApp and would never appear as an explicit instruction. Tests Type 10 cadence: payment received → feedback follow-up implicit task.
notes: R2f — post-delivery feedback implicit task. UNVALIDATED: timing of feedback outreach (same day? 1-2 days after?) and whether this applies to all clients or only Army/major commercial. The testable behaviour is: payment received with no feedback message = feedback follow-up task created.
